texto: I don't go out drinking and stuff like that. My friends say 'Just have one drink, JD.' I say 'What's the point?' I'll go to a club and have a Red Bull, get my buzz. And the next day I feel cool. It's discipline, not just with drinking but a lot of things in life. You've just got to look at the bigger picture.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jermain Defoe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't go out drinking and stuff like that. My friends say 'Just have one drink, JD.' I say 'Wha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, vida, day pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e day.

frase_original: I don't go out drinking and stuff like that. My friends say 'Just have one drink, JD.' I say 'What's the point?' I'll go to a club and have a Red Bull, get my buzz. And the next day I feel cool. It's discipline, not just with drinking but a lot of things in life. You've just got to look at the bigger picture.
